title: Iti Cluster
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1|canon=Iti system}} {{StarCluster |image= |name=Iti Cluster |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the Iti Cluster in grid square T-14, which ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' establishes as part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. |sector=[[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]] |coordinates=T-14 |systems= |suns= |otherplanets= |moons= |stations= |asteroids= |comets= |nebulae= |other= |routes= |quadrants= |species= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= |battles1= |b1= |battles2= |b2= |battles3= |b3= |battles4= |b4= |battles5= |b5= |battles6= |b6= |battles7= |b7= |battles8= |b8= |battles9= |b9= }} {{Quote|You ever hear of Dersonn III? Or the Iti Cluster colonies?''"
"''Weren't they destroyed?''"
"''Right, that's because Hanharr happened.|Mira and the Jedi Exile|''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''}} The '''Iti Cluster''' was a [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]] situated in the [[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]], within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. At one point, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and [[Slaver/Legends|enslaver]] [[Hanharr]], who had previously been [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] himself and harbored a hatred for other living beings, particularly [[Human/Legends|Humans]], caused the destruction of the population of [[Colonist/Legends|colonies]] in the cluster. {{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}} In [[3951 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' to [[3951 BBY]]. the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mira]] relayed Hanharr's involvement with the Iti Cluster to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]], who had previously heard of the destruction of the astronomical object's population. {{Endgame}} ==Behind the scenes== The Iti Cluster was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. In the game, the [[Star/Legends|star]] cluster can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, the Jedi Exile, can have with Mira after the former has made a [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]–aligned choice regarding the latter and Hanharr and after Mira has joined their crew as a result. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion—as well as that the player character makes only light side–aligned choices—and that the conversation took place as described. The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] placed the Iti Cluster in grid square T-14. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} {{ID|Iti system}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Cúmulo Iti }} [[Category:Star clusters in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Star clusters in the Slice]] [[Category:Tammuz sector locations]]
